summary: Frontend-only verification of home-page scroll-lock fixes (welcome animation gesture-trigger + body-fixed lock, paths card stepper one-card-per-gesture, horizontal slide direction, release-after-card-5, no re-engage on back-scroll). All 11 acceptance criteria PASS on Desktop (1920x1080) and Mobile (390x800). 0 console errors. 0 hydration warnings.

tests_executed.T1_welcome_first_wheel_locks_no_scroll: PASS - At scrollY=0, first WheelEvent(deltaY:200) makes document.body computed position become 'fixed' and window.scrollY remains 0. Desktop+Mobile both confirmed.
tests_executed.T2_locked_phase_ignores_additional_wheel: PASS - 5 additional WheelEvents fired in rapid succession during the 2.4s animation: scrollY stays at 0, body remains position:fixed. No skip, no scroll leak.
tests_executed.T3_auto_scroll_to_paths_after_anim: PASS - After ~2.8s wait, paths-scroll-stepper getBoundingClientRect().top = 335px (desktop) / 320px (mobile), well within viewport. The handoff via unlockScroll(targetY) executed correctly.
tests_executed.T4_paths_stepper_lock_engages: PASS - IntersectionObserver engages body lock automatically (computed position:fixed) and dot-1 has aria-current='true' on both desktop and mobile.
tests_executed.T5_one_card_per_gesture_rapid_fire: PASS - 10 dispatched WheelEvents with 30ms gaps advanced exactly ONE card (1->2). The 260ms wheel-end-cooldown correctly absorbs continuous flick streams.
tests_executed.T6_step_through_all_5_cards: PASS - With 450ms gaps between wheel events, cards advanced 2->3->4->5 sequentially. Body remained position:fixed throughout the 5 cards.
tests_executed.T7_release_after_card_5: PASS - The wheel event after reaching card 5 released the lock (body becomes position:relative) and scrolled the page beyond the section (scrollY > 0).
tests_executed.T8_no_relock_on_back_scroll: PASS - After release, programmatic scrollTo(100) keeps body position 'relative' (no re-engage). Backwards-scroll restoration through hero region works (CosmicJourney binds progress to scrollY only when body is NOT fixed).
tests_executed.T9_horizontal_slide_direction: PASS - Off-stage cards have CSS transform matrix(0.92,0,0,0.92,228.96,0) i.e. translateX=228.96 translateY=0 scale=0.92. Active card has transform:none. Confirms x-axis slide only, not vertical.
tests_executed.T10_mobile_viewport_390x800: PASS - Touch swipe-up (touchstart+touchmove dy=-100) triggers the same locked welcome animation. Stepper lock engages identically. One-per-gesture verified on mobile too.
tests_executed.T11_console_errors: PASS - 0 console errors and 0 page errors over the full test cycle on both viewports. No hydration warnings.

critical_code_review_comments: P3 (minor): /app/refgd/components/PathsHorizontalReveal.tsx exposes prop `desktopFallback` in the type signature but the JSDoc says it is now ignored. Recommend removing the prop entirely from the type to avoid confusion / dead-code path.

P3 (minor): scroll-lock.ts uses a module-level `lockCount` ref-count which is correct, but if a route change unmounts BOTH consumers while the lock is engaged, only one decrement runs (CosmicJourney has no unmount-cleanup symmetric to PathsHorizontalReveal lines 304-311). On a Next.js client-side route change while the welcome animation is mid-flight, the body could remain position:fixed on the next page. Consider a useEffect cleanup in CosmicJourney that calls unlockScroll() if phaseRef.current === 'running' on unmount.

P3 (cleanup): CosmicJourney.tsx line 117 reads `document.body.style.position === 'fixed'` to detect another component's lock. Cleaner to call the exported `isScrollLocked()` helper from scroll-lock.ts.

P3 (a11y): The instruction text 'Scroll down once to change path' is helpful, but during the welcome animation the page provides no visible affordance besides the small 'scroll' indicator. Consider adding aria-live region announcing 'Welcome animation playing' for screen readers.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Project is Next.js 15 at /app/refgd. Dev server is on http://localhost:3000/. For TRUE rapid-fire wheel testing, page.mouse.wheel() takes ~1.3s per call which exceeds the 260ms cooldown - use document.dispatchEvent(new WheelEvent('wheel',{deltaY:200,bubbles:true,cancelable:true})) inside page.evaluate with ~30ms gaps. Body lock detection: getComputedStyle(document.body).position === 'fixed'. Welcome animation duration: 2.4s desktop / 2.0s mobile. Touch-trigger via TouchEvent dispatch (touchstart at clientY:600 then touchmove at clientY:500 = 100px finger-up = forward swipe). Active dot has aria-current='true'. Scroll-lock module is reference-counted across CosmicJourney + PathsHorizontalReveal so both can hold a lock simultaneously without stomping. Off-stage card transforms appear as matrix(0.92,0,0,0.92,X,0) where X=+/-228.96 on mobile / larger on desktop, confirming horizontal slide.
rca_of_the_issue: n/a - all 3 bugs reported by user are fixed. The previous setInterval(scrollTo) tug-of-war (which caused 'page slightly scrolls then snaps back') has been replaced with body position:fixed (a single CSS state, no rAF loop). One-card-per-gesture is enforced by a quietGap-based cooldown (260ms desktop / 80ms reduce-motion) keyed on a refs not closure-locals so dev StrictMode double-mount doesn't reset state mid-flick. Welcome animation is now time-based (2.4s) rather than scroll-driven, with body locked during play, satisfying the user's 'scroll triggers animation but page must not scroll' requirement.
